text:
Olympians have been caught flogging expensive souvenirs online after receiving free phones at theParis 2024 Olympicsthis summer.

There are estimated to be over 17,000 athletes participating at the Games and each athlete was gifted a free Samsung phone on their arrival in the Olympic village.

Samsung manufactured a limited edition of Galaxy Z Flip 6 phone for the athletes to use while they are in France, which features a gold finish, custom leather case, unique platina and more.

It has been exclusively designed for the Olympics 2024 athletes and was not released for the general public to buy, making it a coveted item for fans desperate to get their hands on the latest phone.

However, Samsung’s move - which would have cost them around £16.3million in potential sales - may have backfired after it emerged several athletes were selling their phones on online auction websites eBay and Marktplaats.

Multiple listings can be found of the Samsung phone for sale on the website, and because it is not available to buy anywhere else, athletes have been busy hiking up the price.



Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

The listing prices range between £5,096 ($6,500) and £7,840 ($10,000), while there are alternatives with a lower starting price - presumably as a method of creating a bidding war.

The normal retail price of a Galaxy Z Flip 6 phone with 512GB storage is £955 ($1,219), but the foldable edition of the phone appears to be catching the eye of tech collectors.

Team GB athletes Duncan Scott, Bejamin Proud, Bryony Page, Oliver Wynne-Griffith and Tom George were all seen taking victory selfies on the podium with their phones on Day 7 of the Olympics after they won their respective events.

But it seems some other athletes are keen for a quick boost to their bank accounts by cashing in on the high-tech gadget phone.



Technically, Olympians are not prohibited from selling the limited edition phones. But it has reflected badly on the sponsor, who experienced a rise in sales from the use of selfies on the medal podium, that people are keen to offload them.

There may be some sympathy for Olympians, who do not tend to earn the same salaries as other sport stars at the top of their respective profession. While some Olympians can earn as much as £600,000 for winning a gold medal, this isn't the case in every discipline and the prize pot can vary by country.

The reality is that some athletes will never have a chance to reach the podium, and selling a phone as valuable as the Samsung could contribute to their living costs during the Games and beyond.

title: Olympic athletes selling rare Paris 2024 gifts for £8,000 on eBay | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Joe Krishnan
description: Olympians taking part in the Paris 2024 Games have been spotted flogging their expensive gift online.
